steps.targetDesc: macOS Dock — the bar of icons at the bottom of the screen
steps.expectedState: Dock becomes visible if it was hidden
steps.why: The Dock is the fastest way to launch Safari
steps.pitfall: Don't confuse the Dock with the Menu Bar at the top

steps.targetDesc: Safari icon — a blue compass rose icon labeled "Safari" in the Dock
steps.why: Identifying the correct icon prevents opening the wrong browser
steps.pitfall: Chrome and Firefox icons look different but sit nearby; don't click those

steps.targetDesc: Safari icon in the Dock (single left-click)
steps.expectedState: Safari launches; its icon bounces in the Dock and a dot appears beneath it
steps.why: A single click is all that's needed to open an app from the Dock
steps.pitfall: Don't double-click — that opens two Safari windows

steps.expectedState: Safari's window appears with your homepage or a new tab page
steps.why: Allows the app to fully initialize before interacting
steps.pitfall: Don't click repeatedly if it's slow; duplicate windows may open

steps.targetDesc: Menu Bar at the top-left of the screen
steps.expectedState: The word **"Safari"** appears in bold next to the Apple () menu
steps.why: Verifies Safari is the active foreground application
steps.pitfall: If another app name shows, click anywhere inside the Safari window to focus it
